m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the WRONG statement

Options.OptionText: Early versions of the S language contain functions for statistical modeling
The book Programming with Data by John Chambers documents S version of the language
In 1993 Bell Labs gave StatSci (later Insightful Corp.) an exclusive license to develop and sell the S language
All of the mentioned View Answer

m_Item1.Explanation: Insightful sold its implementation of the S language under the product name S-PLUS.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In 2004, ________ purchased the S language from Lucent for $2 million.
m_Item1.Explanation: TIBCO is the current owner of the S language and is its exclusive developer.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In 1991, R was created by Ross Ihaka and Robert Gentleman in the Department of Statistics at the University of _________
m_Item1.Explanation: In 1993 the first announcement of R was made to the public. Ross‘s and Robert‘s experience developing R is documented in a 1996 paper in the Journal of Computational and Graphical Statistics.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the wrong statement :

Options.OptionText: R is a language for data analysis and graphics
K is language for statistical modelling and graphics
One key limitation of the S language was that it was only available in a commercial package, S-PLUS

m_Item1.Explanation: In 1996, a public mailing list was created (the R-help and R-devel lists) and in 1997 the R Core Group was formed, containing some people associated with S and S-PLUS.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Finally, in _________ R version 1.0.0 was released to the public.
m_Item1.Explanation: Source code for the entire R system is accessible to anyone who wanted to tinker with it.

m_Item1.QuestionText: R is technically much closer to the Scheme language than it is to the original _____ language.
m_Item1.Explanation: R‘s semantics, while superficially similar to S, are quite different.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The R-help and _____ mailing lists have been highly active for over a decade now
m_Item1.Explanation: There is considerable activity on web sites like Stack Overflow as well.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following describes R language ?
m_Item1.Explanation: A major advantage that R has over many other statistical packages and is that it‘s free.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The copyright for the primary source code for R is held by the ______ Foundation.
m_Item1.Explanation: It is published under the GNU General Public License version.

m_Item1.QuestionText: They primary R system is available from the ______
m_Item1.Explanation: CRAN stands for Comprehensive R Archive Network.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the wrong statement :

Options.OptionText: Key feature of R was that its syntax is very similar to S
R runs only on Windows computing platform and operating system
R has been reported to be running on modern tablets, phones, PDAs, and game consoles

m_Item1.Explanation: R runs on almost any standard computing platform and operating system.

m_Item1.QuestionText: R functionality is divided into a number of ________
m_Item1.Explanation: CRAN also hosts many add-on packages that can be used to extend the functionality of R.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The _________ R system contains, among other things, the base package which is required to run R and
m_Item1.Explanation: base package in R contains the most fundamental functions.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the wrong statement :

Options.OptionText: One nice feature that R shares with many popular open source projects is frequent releases
S‘s base graphics system allows for very fine control over essentially every aspect of a plot or graph

m_Item1.Explanation: R has maintained the original S philosophy, which is that it provides a language that is both useful for interactive work, but contains a powerful programming language for developing new tools.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a base package for R language ?
m_Item1.Explanation: The other packages contained in the ―base‖ system include utils, stats, datasets, graphics,grDevices, grid, methods, parallel, compiler, splines, tcltk, stats4.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is ―Recommended‖ package in R ?
m_Item1.Explanation: "Recommended" packages also include boot, class, cluster, codetools, foreign, KernSmooth, lattice, mgcv, nlme, rpart, survival, MASS, nnet, Matrix.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How many packages exist in R language for statistics ?
m_Item1.Explanation: There are over 4000 packages on CRAN that have been developed by users and programmers around the world.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Advanced users can write ___ code to manipulate R objects directly.
m_Item1.Explanation: For computationally-intensive tasks, C, C++ and Fortran code can be linked and called at run time.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is used for Statistical analysis in R language ?
m_Item1.Explanation: RStudio is a web application framework for R.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The most convenient way to use R is at a graphics workstation running a ________ system.
m_Item1.Explanation: Most classical statistics and much of the latest methodology is available for use with R.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the wrong statement :

Options.OptionText: Setting up a workstation to take full advantage of the customizable features of R is a straightforward thing
q() is used to quit the R program
R has an inbuilt help facility similar to the man facility of UNIX

m_Item1.Explanation: help command is used for knowing details of particular command in R.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is default prompt for UNIX environment ?
m_Item1.Explanation: When you use the R program it issues a prompt when it expects input commands.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following will start the R program ?
m_Item1.Explanation: At this point R commands may be issued.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the wrong statement :

Options.OptionText: Windows versions of R have other optional help system also
The help.search command (alternatively ??) allows searching for help in various ways
R is case insensitive as are most UNIX based packages, so A and a are different symbols and would refer to different variables

m_Item1.Explanation: R is an expression language with a very simple syntax.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statement is alternative to ?solve
m_Item1.Explanation: help is used to get more information on any specific named function.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Elementary commands in R consist of either _______ or assignments.
m_Item1.Explanation: If an expression is given as a command, it is evaluated, printed (unless specifically made invisible), and the value is lost.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If a command is not complete at the end of a line, R will give a different prompt, by default it is :
m_Item1.Explanation: Comments can be put almost anywhere, starting with a hashmark (‗#‘), everything to the end of the line is a comment.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Command lines entered at the console are limited to about ________ bytes.
m_Item1.Explanation: Elementary commands can be grouped together into one compound expression by braces (‗{‘ and ‗}‘).

m_Item1.QuestionText: _____ text editor provides more general support mechanisms via ESS for working interactively with R.
m_Item1.Explanation: The recall and editing capabilities under UNIX are highly customizable.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The ______ operator allows you to string operations in a left-to-right fashion.
m_Item1.Explanation: The pipeline operator %>% is very handy for stringing together multiple dplyr functions in a sequence of operations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the wrong statement :

Options.OptionText: Statements cannot be grouped together using braces '{‘ and '}‘
Computation in R consists of sequentially evaluating statements
Single statements are evaluated when a new line is typed at the end of the syntactically complete statement

m_Item1.Explanation: Blocks are not evaluated until a new line is entered after the closing brace.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the correct statement :

Options.OptionText: Statements, such as x<-1:10 or mean(y), can be separated by either a semi-colon or a new line
Whenever the evaluator is presented with a syntactically complete statement that statement is evaluated and the value returned
Computation in R consists of sequentially evaluating statements

m_Item1.Explanation: Both semicolons and new lines can be used to separate statements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: R has ________ basic indexing operators.
m_Item1.Explanation: R contains several constructs which allow access to individual elements or subsets through indexing operations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will be the output of following code snippet ? > paste("a", "b", se = ":")
m_Item1.Explanation: With the paste() function, the arguments sep and collapse must be named explicitly and in full if the default values are not going to be used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The only environment without a parent is the ________ environment.
m_Item1.Explanation: Every environment has a parent environment and it is possible for an environment to have multiple " children".

m_Item1.QuestionText: The ________ for R are the main feature that make it different from the original S language
m_Item1.Explanation: This function never actually uses the argument b, so calling f(2) will not produce an error because the 2 gets positionally matched to a.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the correct statement :

Options.OptionText: An environment is a collection of (symbol, value) pairs, i.e. x is a symbol and 3.14 might be its value
If the value of a symbol is not found in the environment in which a function was defined, then the search is continued in the child environment
After the top-level environment, the search continues down the search list until we hit the parent environment

m_Item1.Explanation: Every environment has a parent environment and it is possible for an environment to have multiple  "children".

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the wrong statement :

Options.OptionText: The primary task of debugging any R code is correctly diagnosing what the problem is
R provides only two tools to help you with debugging your code
print statement can be used for debugging purpose

m_Item1.Explanation: R provides a number of tools to help you with debugging your code.

m_Item1.QuestionText: ________ allows you to insert debugging code into a function a specific places
m_Item1.Explanation: The function call stack is the sequence of functions that was called before the error occurred.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Point out the correct statement :

Options.OptionText: R comes with a set of pseudo-random number generators
Random number generators cannot be used to model random inputs
Statistical procedure does not require random number generation

m_Item1.Explanation: R allows you to simulate from well known probability distributions like the Normal, Poisson, and binomial.

m_Item1.QuestionText: ______ evaluate the cumulative distribution function for a Normal distribution
m_Item1.Explanation: p stands for cumulative distribution.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function is used to concatenate strings in R?
m_Item1.Explanation: The paste() function in R is used to concatenate strings, whereas merge() and join() are typically used for data frame operations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you compute the mean of a numerical vector 'x' in R?
m_Item1.Explanation: mean(x) is the function used to calculate the average value of elements within a numerical vector in R.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which argument would you use with the plot() function to add a title to your graph?
m_Item1.Explanation: In R, the main= argument is used in the plot() function to add a main title to the graph.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What function would you use to randomly shuffle a vector in R?
m_Item1.Explanation: The sample() function is used to randomly shuffle the elements of a vector in R.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which version of R is recommended for installing on a machine for use with the latest RStudio as of October 2023?
m_Item1.Explanation: RStudio typically recommends using the latest stable version of R for compatibility and support. As of October 2023, R version 4.2.3 is the latest stable release.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary website where you can download the R software?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN) is the primary website for downloading R, which is located at 'cran.r-project.org'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When installing R on Windows, which of the following steps is part of the installation process?

Options.OptionText: You must manually set environment variables in the command line.
You need to compile the source code during installation.
Selecting the option to customize components during the installation wizard.
Removing previous versions of R before installing a new one.

m_Item1.Explanation: During the installation of R on Windows, the installation wizard offers an option to customize components that you might want to install, such as 32-bit or 64-bit versions.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a major difference in the primary application areas between Python and R?

Options.OptionText: Python is primarily used for statistical analysis while R excels in web development.
Python is widely used for data science and machine learning, whereas R is typically used for pervasive statistical analysis.
R is used for general-purpose programming whereas Python is dedicated to data visualization.
R is used for software development, whereas Python is used for database management.

m_Item1.Explanation: Python and R are often mentioned together, but Python is renowned for its general-purpose programming and machine learning capabilities, while R is especially employed for statistical analysis and visualization tasks.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do Python and R differ in terms of community support and libraries?

Options.OptionText: Python has more extensive libraries for statistical modeling compared to R.
R has a smaller, growing community compared to Python, with more libraries for machine learning.
Both have an equal number of libraries for all purposes, but Python's community support is older.
Python generally has a larger community and more diverse libraries compared to R, which has more specialized libraries for statistical analysis.

m_Item1.Explanation: Python boasts a larger and more diverse community with libraries spanning various domains, while R's libraries are more focused on statistical computing and analysis.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In terms of usability for beginners, what is a common view of the learning curve when comparing Python and R?

Options.OptionText: R is considered easier for beginners and has a more intuitive syntax than Python.
Python is generally seen as having a gentler learning curve for beginners due to its readability and simplicity.
Both Python and R have equally steep learning curves due to complex syntax.
Neither Python nor R is suitable for beginners due to their complexity.

m_Item1.Explanation: Python is often recommended for beginners because of its simple and readable syntax, compared to R's steeper learning curve due to its syntax and statistical focus.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a distinctive feature of R programming in statistical analysis compared to Python?

Options.OptionText: R has built-in functions that offer superior statistical testing and plotting capabilities.
Python's libraries are pre-built for statistical analysis, making it better than R.
R lacks any visualization capabilities, whereas Python has advanced graphical abilities.
Both languages rely solely on third-party libraries for statistical analysis capabilities.

m_Item1.Explanation: R programming is particularly strong in statistical analysis, featuring built-in functions and packages that excel in statistical testing and plotting, which are often more readily available and specific than those in Python.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function in R can you use to get documentation for a specific function or package?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'help()' function in R is used to access documentation for specific functions or packages. You can use it by calling help('function_name').

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the '?' operator in R?

Options.OptionText: To initiate a help search for a topic
To execute a block of code conditionally

m_Item1.Explanation: In R, using the '?' operator followed by the name of the function or topic (e.g., '?mean') opens the help page related to that function or topic, thus initiating a help search.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which command would you use to search for resources related to a particular topic in R using keywords?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'help.search('topic')' function in R searches for help files that use the specified keyword or topic name. It searches through the descriptions of packages and gives a list of potential matches.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following code editors is specifically designed with features to support R programming, such as syntax highlighting, R script execution, and package management?
m_Item1.Explanation: RStudio is a powerful IDE specifically tailored for R programming. It includes features like syntax highlighting, R script execution, and integrated support for R packages, making it an ideal choice for R developers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which feature in RStudio allows users to easily manage R packages including installing, updating, and uninstalling them?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'Packages Pane' in RStudio is specifically designed for managing R packages. It allows users to install, update, and uninstall packages conveniently within the RStudio interface.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In RStudio, what is the primary purpose of the 'Console' pane?

Options.OptionText: To edit scripts with syntax highlighting
To write and run code in Markdown format

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'Console' pane in RStudio is used for executing R code interactively. It allows users to type and run R commands directly, providing immediate feedback for code execution.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a popular GUI tool for R that helps in creating interactive applications?
m_Item1.Explanation: Shiny is a popular package in R that allows users to build interactive web applications directly from R. It provides a GUI framework for creating applications that can be hosted either locally or online.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary purpose of using R Commander?

Options.OptionText: To provide a basic graph plotting tool
To offer a simple GUI interface for basic statistical analysis
To enable data scraping from web pages

m_Item1.Explanation: R Commander is an R package that provides a simple graphical user interface designed to perform basic statistical and graphical procedures. It is particularly useful for users who prefer not to write R code manually.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which feature distinguishes RStudio from other R GUI tools?

Options.OptionText: It supports code completion and syntax highlighting.
It can only run on Windows operating systems.
It is available solely as a command-line interface.
It exclusively focuses on data visualization.

m_Item1.Explanation: RStudio is a comprehensive integrated development environment (IDE) for R programming. One of its distinguishing features is the support for code completion and syntax highlighting, which improves the coding experience. It is available across multiple platforms, including Windows, macOS, and Linux, and it offers functionalities beyond just data visualization.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the default working directory path in RStudio when a new project is created?

Options.OptionText: The location of RStudio's executable file
The folder where R script files are stored
A directory called 'Projects' inside the user's documents folder

m_Item1.Explanation:
When you create a new project in RStudio, it creates a folder containing:

  • A .Rproj project file

  • Any R scripts or data files you create

By default, RStudio sets the working directory to this project folder, i.e., where the R script files are saved.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which panel in RStudio shows the Environment and History tabs by default?
m_Item1.Explanation: In RStudio, the Environment pane by default contains the Environment and History tabs, where users can see their data frames and past command history.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can a user install a package in RStudio?

Options.OptionText: By selecting 'Install Package' from the Console's dropdown menu
By right-clicking on the Files pane
By editing the R source code directly

m_Item1.Explanation: Packages can be installed in RStudio using the command `install.packages('package-name')` directly in the Console pane.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the 'Projects' feature in RStudio?

Options.OptionText: To organize related files and settings in a single workspace

m_Item1.Explanation: RStudio's 'Projects' feature is designed to manage related files and configurations collectively, making it easier to work on specific datasets or analyses in an organized manner.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function in R is used to display the current objects in the workspace?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ls() function in R is used to list all the objects currently present in the workspace.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the rm() function in R?

Options.OptionText: To restore objects from the workspace
To remove specified objects from the workspace
To read multiple objects from a file
To rename objects in the workspace

m_Item1.Explanation: The rm() function in R is used to remove specified objects from the workspace.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you save the current workspace to an R data file?
m_Item1.Explanation: The save() function is used to save the current workspace to an R data file with an '.RData' extension.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function is used to load an R data file into the current workspace?
m_Item1.Explanation: The load() function is used to load objects from an .RData file into the current workspace.

m_Item1.QuestionText: R comes with a ________ to help you optimize your code and improve its performance.
m_Item1.Explanation: In general, it‘s usually a bad idea to focus on optimizing your code at the very beginning of development.
